title: pilosella glaucescens subsp. kerneri
intro: Pilosella glaucescens subsp. kerneri, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Pilosella kerneri, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to the high mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, Pilosella glaucescens subsp. kerneri has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, leading to several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and boosting the immune system. Pilosella glaucescens subsp. kerneri can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and providing a natural remedy for various health conditions.
